body_txt: One way to make a character look large without looking intimidating is to give them a pear-shaped body: large below the waist (hips, belly, butt), but relatively narrow above the waist (chest, shoulders). Whereas a Top-Heavy Guy with big chest and shoulders and narrow hips looks strong and menacing (though may actually have a gentle personality), a pear-shaped being will almost always be a Gentle Giant . The soft, rounded shapes give a friendly appearance in contrast with Evil Is Angular . The pear shape is sometimes duplicated with the character's head, with chubby cheeks and a large chin but a small cranium. Primarily an animation trope. Very common with various Benevolent Monsters , particularly the Delightful Dragon . Also seen in some large animals like bears , gorillas , or hippos , if they are portrayed as friendly. Might overlap with Big Fun and Fat Comic Relief . Compare Hartman Hips where a pear-shaped body is an indicator of an adult woman, and Impossible Hourglass Figure and Big Beautiful Woman where a thick and curvy figure is played for Fanservice . Contrast Fat Bastard , Tiny-Headed Behemoth .
